interactions.summary: Jiang closes by saying the first Bible was propaganda, but the Yahwist used that opportunity to make living stories that ignite Jewish imagination; Jews, Greeks, and Persians become pillars of Western civilization.

speaker_notes.note: Jiang closes the class and asks if there are more questions; no substantive new student question appears in the focus ref.

claims.claim: The Bible was first constructed as propaganda, but the Yahwist transformed that political occasion into beautiful stories that live as collective memory.
These living biblical stories ignite Jewish imagination and explain Jewish creativity in Jiang's lecture model.
Jews, Greeks, and Persians are presented as the three most creative peoples of the period and as pillars of Western civilization.
claims.temporal_scope: Course transition statement in this lecture.

signature_moments.moment: Propaganda becomes living memory and living memory ignites imagination.
signature_moments.source_phrase: beautiful stories that become living entities, living memories
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It resolves the lecture's tension: a political instrument can become the engine of creativity.

signature_moments.moment: The course moves from Greeks and Jews to Persians as the three creative pillars of Western civilization.
signature_moments.source_phrase: the Jews, the Greeks, and the Persians
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It places this episode in the sequence of Jiang's civilizational genealogy.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Stories that outgrow their political origin and remain active in a people's imagination.
Jiang's grouping of Jews, Greeks, and Persians as the period's three most creative peoples shaping the West.

chronology_notes.note: The source closes Secret History #17 by identifying the next class as Persians after Greeks and Jews.

uncertainty_notes.note: ASR phrase 'Yahwehs, or the J' appears to refer to the Yahwist/J source.
